1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: require 'spec_helper'
4: 
5: RSpec.describe RubyLLM::Provider do
6:   def api_base_cases
7:     {
8:       anthropic: {
9:         provider: RubyLLM::Providers::Anthropic,
10:         key: :anthropic_api_base,
11:         custom: 'https://anthropic-proxy.example.com',
12:         default: 'https://api.anthropic.com'
13:       },
14:       azure: {
15:         provider: RubyLLM::Providers::Azure,
16:         key: :azure_api_base,
17:         custom: 'https://azure-resource.example.com'
18:       },
19:       bedrock: {
20:         provider: RubyLLM::Providers::Bedrock,
21:         key: :bedrock_api_base,
22:         custom: 'https://bedrock-proxy.example.com',
23:         default: 'https://bedrock-runtime.us-east-1.amazonaws.com'
24:       },
25:       deepseek: {
26:         provider: RubyLLM::Providers::DeepSeek,
27:         key: :deepseek_api_base,
28:         custom: 'https://deepseek-proxy.example.com',
29:         default: 'https://api.deepseek.com'
30:       },
31:       gemini: {
32:         provider: RubyLLM::Providers::Gemini,
33:         key: :gemini_api_base,
34:         custom: 'https://gemini-proxy.example.com/v1',
35:         default: 'https://generativelanguage.googleapis.com/v1beta'
36:       },
37:       gpustack: {
38:         provider: RubyLLM::Providers::GPUStack,
39:         key: :gpustack_api_base,
40:         custom: 'https://gpustack.example.com/v1'
41:       },
42:       mistral: {
43:         provider: RubyLLM::Providers::Mistral,
44:         key: :mistral_api_base,
45:         custom: 'https://mistral-proxy.example.com/v1',
46:         default: 'https://api.mistral.ai/v1'
47:       },
48:       ollama: {
49:         provider: RubyLLM::Providers::Ollama,
50:         key: :ollama_api_base,
51:         custom: 'https://ollama.example.com/v1'
52:       },
53:       openai: {
54:         provider: RubyLLM::Providers::OpenAI,
55:         key: :openai_api_base,
56:         custom: 'https://openai-proxy.example.com/v1',
57:         default: 'https://api.openai.com/v1'
58:       },
59:       openrouter: {
60:         provider: RubyLLM::Providers::OpenRouter,
61:         key: :openrouter_api_base,
62:         custom: 'https://openrouter-proxy.example.com/api/v1',
63:         default: 'https://openrouter.ai/api/v1'
64:       },
65:       perplexity: {
66:         provider: RubyLLM::Providers::Perplexity,
67:         key: :perplexity_api_base,
68:         custom: 'https://perplexity-proxy.example.com',
69:         default: 'https://api.perplexity.ai'
70:       },
71:       vertexai: {
72:         provider: RubyLLM::Providers::VertexAI,
73:         key: :vertexai_api_base,
74:         custom: 'https://vertex-proxy.example.com/v1beta1',
75:         default: 'https://us-east1-aiplatform.googleapis.com/v1beta1'
76:       },
77:       xai: {
78:         provider: RubyLLM::Providers::XAI,
79:         key: :xai_api_base,
80:         custom: 'https://xai-proxy.example.com/v1',
81:         default: 'https://api.x.ai/v1'
82:       }
83:     }
84:   end
85: 
86:   def config_for(slug)
87:     RubyLLM::Configuration.new.tap do |config|
88:       case slug
89:       when :anthropic
90:         config.anthropic_api_key = 'anthropic-key'
91:       when :azure
92:         config.azure_api_key = 'azure-key'
93:       when :bedrock
94:         config.bedrock_api_key = 'bedrock-key'
95:         config.bedrock_secret_key = 'bedrock-secret'
96:         config.bedrock_region = 'us-east-1'
97:       when :deepseek
98:         config.deepseek_api_key = 'deepseek-key'
99:       when :gemini
100:         config.gemini_api_key = 'gemini-key'
101:       when :gpustack
102:         config.gpustack_api_key = 'gpustack-key'
103:       when :mistral
104:         config.mistral_api_key = 'mistral-key'
105:       when :ollama
106:         config.ollama_api_key = 'ollama-key'
107:       when :openai
108:         config.openai_api_key = 'openai-key'
109:       when :openrouter
110:         config.openrouter_api_key = 'openrouter-key'
111:       when :perplexity
112:         config.perplexity_api_key = 'perplexity-key'
113:       when :vertexai
114:         config.vertexai_project_id = 'vertex-project'
115:         config.vertexai_location = 'us-east1'
116:       when :xai
117:         config.xai_api_key = 'xai-key'
118:       end
119:     end
120:   end
121: 
122:   describe '.register' do
123:     it 'registers provider configuration options on Configuration' do
124:       provider_key = :test_provider_spec
125:       option_keys = %i[test_provider_api_key test_provider_api_base]
126: 
127:       provider_class = Class.new(described_class) do
128:         class << self
129:           def configuration_options
130:             %i[test_provider_api_key test_provider_api_base]
131:           end
132: 
133:           def configuration_requirements
134:             %i[test_provider_api_key]
135:           end
136:         end
137:       end
138: 
139:       original_providers = described_class.providers.dup
140: 
141:       begin
142:         described_class.register(provider_key, provider_class)
143: 
144:         config = RubyLLM::Configuration.new
145:         option_keys.each do |key|
146:           expect(config).to respond_to(key)
147:           expect(config).to respond_to("#{key}=")
148:         end
149:       ensure
150:         described_class.providers.replace(original_providers)
151:         option_keys.each do |key|
152:           RubyLLM::Configuration.send(:option_keys).delete(key)
153:           RubyLLM::Configuration.send(:defaults).delete(key)
154:           RubyLLM::Configuration.class_eval do
155:             remove_method key if method_defined?(key)
156:             remove_method :"#{key}=" if method_defined?(:"#{key}=")
157:           end
158:         end
159:       end
160:     end
161:   end
162: 
163:   describe '.configured?' do
164:     it 'treats blank required configuration as missing' do
165:       provider_class = Class.new(described_class) do
166:         class << self
167:           def configuration_options
168:             %i[blank_test_api_key]
169:           end
170: 
171:           def configuration_requirements
172:             %i[blank_test_api_key]
173:           end
174:         end
175:       end
176: 
177:       RubyLLM::Configuration.register_provider_options(provider_class.configuration_options)
178:       config = RubyLLM::Configuration.new
179:       config.blank_test_api_key = ''
180: 
181:       expect(provider_class.configured?(config)).to be(false)
182:     ensure
183:       RubyLLM::Configuration.send(:option_keys).delete(:blank_test_api_key)
184:       RubyLLM::Configuration.send(:defaults).delete(:blank_test_api_key)
185:       RubyLLM::Configuration.class_eval do
186:         remove_method :blank_test_api_key if method_defined?(:blank_test_api_key)
187:         remove_method :blank_test_api_key= if method_defined?(:blank_test_api_key=)
188:       end
189:     end
190:   end
191: 
192:   describe 'provider configuration schema' do
193:     it 'keeps requirements as a subset of declared configuration options' do
194:       described_class.providers.each_value do |provider_class|
195:         missing = provider_class.configuration_requirements - provider_class.configuration_options
196:         expect(missing).to be_empty, "#{provider_class.name} is missing options for requirements: #{missing.inspect}"
197:       end
198:     end
199: 
200:     it 'exposes aggregated provider options through Configuration' do
201:       expect(RubyLLM::Configuration.options).to include(
202:         :openrouter_api_base,
203:         :deepseek_api_base,
204:         :ollama_api_key
205:       )
206: 
207:       expect(RubyLLM::Configuration.options).to include(
208:         :request_timeout,
209:         :model_registry_class
210:       )
211:     end
212:   end
213: 
214:   context 'with API base configuration' do
215:     it 'covers every registered provider' do
216:       expect(api_base_cases.keys).to match_array(described_class.providers.keys)
217:     end
218: 
219:     it 'registers an API base option for every provider' do
220:       expected_options = api_base_cases.values.map { |data| data[:key] }
221: 
222:       expect(RubyLLM::Configuration.options).to include(*expected_options)
223:     end
224: 
225:     it 'uses the configured API base for every provider' do
226:       api_base_cases.each do |slug, data|
227:         config = config_for(slug)
228:         config.public_send("#{data[:key]}=", data[:custom])
229: 
230:         expect(data[:provider].new(config).api_base).to eq(data[:custom])
231:       end
232:     end
233: 
234:     it 'keeps existing defaults for providers with built-in endpoints' do
235:       api_base_cases.each do |slug, data|
236:         next unless data[:default]
237: 
238:         expect(data[:provider].new(config_for(slug)).api_base).to eq(data[:default])
239:       end
240:     end
241:   end
242: 
243:   describe 'protocol resolution' do
244:     let(:provider) { RubyLLM::Providers::OpenAI.new(config_for(:openai)) }
245:     let(:model) { instance_double(RubyLLM::Model::Info, id: 'gpt-5.4') }
246: 
247:     it 'defaults to the first declared protocol' do
248:       expect(RubyLLM::Providers::OpenAI.default_protocol).to eq(:responses)
249:       expect(RubyLLM::Providers::Mistral.default_protocol).to eq(:chat_completions)
250:     end
251: 
252:     it 'routes through protocol_for by default' do
253:       expect(provider.send(:resolve_protocol, nil, model)).to eq(RubyLLM::Protocols::Responses)
254:     end
255: 
256:     it 'routes chat-completions-only models away from the default' do
257:       audio = instance_double(RubyLLM::Model::Info, id: 'gpt-audio-mini')
258: 
259:       expect(provider.send(:resolve_protocol, nil, audio)).to eq(RubyLLM::Protocols::ChatCompletions)
260:     end
261: 
262:     it 'prefers the configured protocol over routing' do
263:       config = config_for(:openai)
264:       config.openai_protocol = :chat_completions
265: 
266:       provider = RubyLLM::Providers::OpenAI.new(config)
267: 
268:       expect(provider.send(:resolve_protocol, nil, model)).to eq(RubyLLM::Protocols::ChatCompletions)
269:     end
270: 
271:     it 'prefers an explicit protocol over the configured one' do
272:       config = config_for(:openai)
273:       config.openai_protocol = :chat_completions
274: 
275:       provider = RubyLLM::Providers::OpenAI.new(config)
276: 
277:       expect(provider.send(:resolve_protocol, :responses, model)).to eq(RubyLLM::Protocols::Responses)
278:     end
279: 
280:     it 'raises on protocols the provider does not speak' do
281:       expect do
282:         provider.send(:resolve_protocol, :gemini, model)
283:       end.to raise_error(RubyLLM::Error, /gemini is not a protocol of OpenAI\. Available: responses, chat_completions/)
284:     end
285:   end
286: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: require 'spec_helper'
4: 
5: RSpec.describe RubyLLM::Configuration do
6:   describe 'DSL defaults' do
7:     subject(:config) { described_class.new }
8: 
9:     it 'applies core default values' do
10:       expect(config.model_registry_class).to eq('Model')
11:       expect(config.use_new_acts_as).to be(true)
12:       expect(config.model_registry_source).to be_nil
13:       expect(config.request_timeout).to eq(300)
14:       expect(config.max_retries).to eq(3)
15:       expect(config.retry_interval).to eq(0.1)
16:       expect(config.retry_backoff_factor).to eq(2)
17:       expect(config.retry_interval_randomness).to eq(0.5)
18:       expect(config.tool_concurrency).to be(false)
19:       expect(config.deprecation_behavior).to eq(:warn)
20:       expect(config.faraday_adapter).to eq(:net_http)
21:     end
22: 
23:     it 'exposes a discoverable options API' do
24:       expect(described_class.options).to include(
25:         :request_timeout,
26:         :tool_concurrency,
27:         :default_model,
28:         :model_registry_file,
29:         :use_new_acts_as,
30:         :openai_api_key,
31:         :openrouter_api_base
32:       )
33:     end
34: 
35:     it 'normalizes blank strings to nil' do
36:       config.openai_api_base = ''
37:       config.anthropic_api_key = " \t\n"
38: 
39:       expect(config.openai_api_base).to be_nil
40:       expect(config.anthropic_api_key).to be_nil
41:     end
42: 
43:     it 'preserves non-blank strings' do
44:       config.openai_api_base = 'https://openai-compatible.example.com/v1'
45: 
46:       expect(config.openai_api_base).to eq('https://openai-compatible.example.com/v1')
47:     end
48: 
49:     it 'warns but preserves log_regexp_timeout when regexp timeouts are unsupported' do
50:       allow(Regexp).to receive(:respond_to?).and_call_original
51:       allow(Regexp).to receive(:respond_to?).with(:timeout).and_return(false)
52:       allow(RubyLLM.logger).to receive(:warn)
53: 
54:       config.log_regexp_timeout = 5.0
55: 
56:       expect(config.log_regexp_timeout).to eq(5.0)
57:       expect(RubyLLM.logger).to have_received(:warn).with(
58:         "log_regexp_timeout is not supported on Ruby #{RUBY_VERSION}"
59:       )
60:     end
61:   end
62: end

<content>
1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: require 'dotenv/load'
4: require 'ruby_llm'
5: require 'json'
6: require 'json-schema'
7: require 'fileutils'
8: 
9: desc 'Update models, docs, and aliases'
10: task models: ['models:update', 'models:docs', 'models:aliases']
11: 
12: namespace :models do
13:   desc 'Update available models from providers (API keys needed)'
14:   task :update do
15:     puts 'Configuring RubyLLM...'
16:     configure_from_env
17:     refresh_models
18:     display_model_stats
19:   end
20: 
21:   desc 'Generate available models documentation'
22:   task :docs do
23:     FileUtils.mkdir_p('docs/_reference')
24:     output = generate_models_markdown
25:     File.write('docs/_reference/available-models.md', output)
26:     puts 'Generated docs/_reference/available-models.md'
27:   end
28: 
29:   desc 'Generate model aliases from registry'
30:   task :aliases do
31:     generate_aliases
32:   end
33: end
34: 
35: # Keep aliases:generate for backwards compatibility
36: namespace :aliases do
37:   task generate: ['models:aliases']
38: end
39: 
40: def configure_from_env
41:   RubyLLM.configure do |config|
42:     config.anthropic_api_key = ENV.fetch('ANTHROPIC_API_KEY', nil)
43:     config.azure_api_base = ENV.fetch('AZURE_API_BASE', nil)
44:     config.azure_api_key = ENV.fetch('AZURE_API_KEY', nil)
45:     config.deepseek_api_key = ENV.fetch('DEEPSEEK_API_KEY', nil)
46:     config.gemini_api_key = ENV.fetch('GEMINI_API_KEY', nil)
47:     config.mistral_api_key = ENV.fetch('MISTRAL_API_KEY', nil)
48:     config.openai_api_key = ENV.fetch('OPENAI_API_KEY', nil)
49:     config.openrouter_api_key = ENV.fetch('OPENROUTER_API_KEY', nil)
50:     config.perplexity_api_key = ENV.fetch('PERPLEXITY_API_KEY', nil)
51:     config.vertexai_location = ENV.fetch('GOOGLE_CLOUD_LOCATION', nil)
52:     config.vertexai_project_id = ENV.fetch('GOOGLE_CLOUD_PROJECT', nil)
53:     config.xai_api_key = ENV.fetch('XAI_API_KEY', nil)
54:     configure_bedrock(config)
55:     config.request_timeout = 30
56:   end
57: end
58: 
59: def configure_bedrock(config)
60:   config.bedrock_api_key = ENV.fetch('AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID', nil)
61:   config.bedrock_secret_key = ENV.fetch('AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY', nil)
62:   config.bedrock_region = ENV.fetch('AWS_REGION', nil)
63:   config.bedrock_session_token = ENV.fetch('AWS_SESSION_TOKEN', nil)
64: end
65: 
66: def refresh_models
67:   existing_models = RubyLLM::Models.read_from_json
68:   initial_count = existing_models.size
69:   puts "Refreshing models (#{initial_count} cached)..."
70: 
71:   models = RubyLLM.models.refresh!
72: 
73:   if models.all.empty? && initial_count.zero?
74:     puts 'Error: Failed to fetch models.'
75:     exit(1)
76:   else
77:     existing_data = sorted_models_data(existing_models)
78:     new_data = sorted_models_data(models.all)
79: 
80:     if new_data == existing_data && initial_count.positive?
81:       puts 'Warning: Model list unchanged.'
82:     else
83:       puts 'Validating models...'
84:       validate_models!(models)
85: 
86:       puts "Saving models.json (#{models.all.size} models)"
87:       models.save_to_json
88:     end
89:   end
90: 
91:   @models = models
92: end
93: 
94: def sorted_models_data(models)
95:   models.map(&:to_h)
96:         .sort_by { |model| [model[:provider].to_s, model[:id].to_s] }
97: end
98: 
99: def validate_models!(models)
100:   schema_path = RubyLLM::Models.schema_file
101:   models_data = models.all.map(&:to_h)
102: 
103:   validation_errors = JSON::Validator.fully_validate(schema_path, models_data)
104: 
105:   unless validation_errors.empty?
106:     # Save failed models for inspection
107:     failed_path = File.expand_path('../ruby_llm/models.failed.json', __dir__)
108:     File.write(failed_path, JSON.pretty_generate(models_data))
109: 
110:     puts 'ERROR: Models validation failed:'
111:     puts "\nValidation errors:"
112:     validation_errors.first(10).each { |error| puts "  - #{error}" }
113:     puts "  ... and #{validation_errors.size - 10} more errors" if validation_errors.size > 10
114:     puts "-> Failed models saved to: #{failed_path}"
115:     exit(1)
116:   end
117: 
118:   puts '✓ Models validation passed'
119: end
120: 
121: def display_model_stats
122:   puts "\nModel count:"
123:   provider_counts = @models.all.group_by(&:provider).transform_values(&:count)
124: 
125:   RubyLLM::Provider.providers.each do |sym, provider_class|
126:     name = provider_class.name
127:     count = provider_counts[sym.to_s] || 0
128:     status = status(sym)
129:     puts "  #{name}: #{count} models #{status}"
130:   end
131: 
132:   puts 'Refresh complete.'
133: end
134: 
135: def status(provider_sym)
136:   provider_class = RubyLLM::Provider.providers[provider_sym]
137:   if provider_class.local?
138:     ' (LOCAL - SKIP)'
139:   elsif provider_class.configured?(RubyLLM.config)
140:     ' (OK)'
141:   else
142:     ' (NOT CONFIGURED)'
143:   end
144: end
145: 
146: def generate_models_markdown
147:   models = RubyLLM.models.all
148:   total_models = models.count
149:   provider_count = models.map(&:provider).uniq.count
150:   generated_on = Time.now.utc.strftime('%Y-%m-%d')
151: 
152:   <<~MARKDOWN
153:     ---
154:     layout: default
155:     title: Available Models
156:     nav_order: 1
157:     description: Browse #{total_models} AI models across #{provider_count} providers (not including local providers). Updated #{generated_on}.
158:     redirect_from:
159:       - /guides/available-models
160:     ---
161: 
162:     # {{ page.title }}
163:     {: .no_toc }
164: 
165:     {{ page.description }}
166:     {: .fs-6 .fw-300 }
167: 
168:     ## Table of contents
169:     {: .no_toc .text-delta }
170: 
171:     1. TOC
172:     {:toc}
173: 
174:     ---
175: 
176:     _Model information enriched by [models.dev](https://models.dev) and our custom code._
177: 
178:     Can't find a newly released model? Refresh your registry:
179: 
180:     ```ruby
181:     # Plain Ruby
182:     RubyLLM.models.refresh!
183: 
184:     # Rails
185:     Model.refresh!
186:     ```
187: 
188:     See [Model Registry: Refreshing the Registry]({% link _advanced/models.md %}#refreshing-the-registry).
189: 
190:     ## Models by Provider
191: 
192:     #{generate_provider_sections}
193: 
194:     ## Models by Capability
195: 
196:     #{generate_capability_sections}
197: 
198:     ## Models by Modality
199: 
200:     #{generate_modality_sections}
201:   MARKDOWN
202: end
203: 
204: def generate_provider_sections
205:   RubyLLM::Provider.providers.filter_map do |provider, provider_class|
206:     models = RubyLLM.models.by_provider(provider)
207:     next if models.none?
208: 
209:     <<~PROVIDER
210:       ### #{provider_class.name} (#{models.count})
211: 
212:       #{models_table(models)}
213:     PROVIDER
214:   end.join("\n\n")
215: end
216: 
217: def generate_capability_sections
218:   capabilities = {
219:     'Function Calling' => RubyLLM.models.select(&:function_calling?),
220:     'Structured Output' => RubyLLM.models.select(&:structured_output?),
221:     'Streaming' => RubyLLM.models.select { |m| m.capabilities.include?('streaming') },
222:     'Batch Processing' => RubyLLM.models.select { |m| m.capabilities.include?('batch') }
223:   }
224: 
225:   capabilities.filter_map do |capability, models|
226:     next if models.none?
227: 
228:     <<~CAPABILITY
229:       ### #{capability} (#{models.count})
230: 
231:       #{models_table(models)}
232:     CAPABILITY
233:   end.join("\n\n")
234: end
235: 
236: def generate_modality_sections # rubocop:disable Metrics/PerceivedComplexity
237:   sections = []
238: 
239:   vision_models = RubyLLM.models.select { |m| (m.modalities.input || []).include?('image') }
240:   if vision_models.any?
241:     sections << <<~SECTION
242:       ### Vision Models (#{vision_models.count})
243: 
244:       Models that can process images:
245: 
246:       #{models_table(vision_models)}
247:     SECTION
248:   end
249: 
250:   audio_models = RubyLLM.models.select { |m| (m.modalities.input || []).include?('audio') }
251:   if audio_models.any?
252:     sections << <<~SECTION
253:       ### Audio Input Models (#{audio_models.count})
254: 
255:       Models that can process audio:
256: 
257:       #{models_table(audio_models)}
258:     SECTION
259:   end
260: 
261:   pdf_models = RubyLLM.models.select { |m| (m.modalities.input || []).include?('pdf') }
262:   if pdf_models.any?
263:     sections << <<~SECTION
264:       ### PDF Models (#{pdf_models.count})
265: 
266:       Models that can process PDF documents:
267: 
268:       #{models_table(pdf_models)}
269:     SECTION
270:   end
271: 
272:   embedding_models = RubyLLM.models.select { |m| (m.modalities.output || []).include?('embeddings') }
273:   if embedding_models.any?
274:     sections << <<~SECTION
275:       ### Embedding Models (#{embedding_models.count})
276: 
277:       Models that generate embeddings:
278: 
279:       #{models_table(embedding_models)}
280:     SECTION
281:   end
282: 
283:   sections.join("\n\n")
284: end
285: 
286: def models_table(models)
287:   return '*No models found*' if models.none?
288: 
289:   headers = ['Model', 'Provider', 'I/O', 'Capabilities', 'Context', 'Max Output', 'Standard Pricing (per 1M tokens)']
290:   alignment = [':--', ':--', ':--', ':--', '--:', '--:', ':--']
291: 
292:   rows = models.sort_by { |m| [m.provider, m.name] }.map do |model|
293:     pricing = standard_pricing_display(model)
294: 
295:     [
296:       model.id,
297:       model.provider,
298:       modalities_display(model),
299:       list_display(model.capabilities),
300:       model.context_window || '-',
301:       model.max_output_tokens || '-',
302:       pricing
303:     ]
304:   end
305: 
306:   table = []
307:   table << "| #{headers.join(' | ')} |"
308:   table << "| #{alignment.join(' | ')} |"
309: 
310:   rows.each do |row|
311:     table << "| #{row.join(' | ')} |"
312:   end
313: 
314:   table.join("\n")
315: end
316: 
317: def modalities_display(model)
318:   input_modalities = list_display(model.modalities.input)
319:   output_modalities = list_display(model.modalities.output)
320:   "In: #{input_modalities}; Out: #{output_modalities}"
321: end
322: 
323: def list_display(values)
324:   items = Array(values).compact.map(&:to_s).reject(&:empty?)
325:   return '-' if items.empty?
326: 
327:   items.join(', ')
328: end
329: 
330: def standard_pricing_display(model)
331:   pricing_data = model.pricing.to_h[:text_tokens]&.dig(:standard) || {}
332:   parts = [
333:     pricing_part(pricing_data, :input_per_million, 'In'),
334:     pricing_part(pricing_data, :output_per_million, 'Out'),
335:     pricing_part(pricing_data, %i[cache_read_input_per_million cached_input_per_million], 'Cache Read'),
336:     pricing_part(pricing_data, %i[cache_write_input_per_million cache_creation_input_per_million], 'Cache Write')
337:   ].compact
338: 
339:   return parts.join(', ') if parts.any?
340: 
341:   '-'
342: end
343: 
344: def pricing_part(pricing_data, key, label)
345:   key = Array(key).find { |candidate| pricing_data[candidate] }
346:   return unless key
347: 
348:   "#{label}: $#{format('%.2f', pricing_data[key])}"
349: end
350: 
351: def generate_aliases # rubocop:disable Metrics/AbcSize, Metrics/CyclomaticComplexity, Metrics/PerceivedComplexity
352:   models = Hash.new { |h, k| h[k] = [] }
353: 
354:   RubyLLM.models.all.each do |model|
355:     models[model.provider] << model.id
356:   end
357: 
358:   aliases = {}
359: 
360:   # OpenAI models
361:   models['openai'].each do |model|
362:     openrouter_model = "openai/#{model}"
363:     azure_model = models['azure'].include?(model) ? model : nil
364:     next unless models['openrouter'].include?(openrouter_model)
365: 
366:     alias_key = model.gsub('-latest', '')
367:     aliases[alias_key] = {
368:       'openai' => model,
369:       'openrouter' => openrouter_model
370:     }
371:     aliases[alias_key]['azure'] = azure_model if azure_model
372:   end
373: 
374:   anthropic_latest = group_anthropic_models_by_base_name(models['anthropic'])
375: 
376:   anthropic_latest.each do |base_name, latest_model|
377:     openrouter_variants = [
378:       "anthropic/#{base_name}",
379:       "anthropic/#{base_name.gsub(/-(\d)/, '.\1')}",
380:       "anthropic/#{base_name.gsub(/claude-(\d+)-(\d+)/, 'claude-\1.\2')}",
381:       "anthropic/#{base_name.gsub(/(\d+)-(\d+)/, '\1.\2')}"
382:     ]
383: 
384:     openrouter_model = openrouter_variants.find { |v| models['openrouter'].include?(v) }
385:     bedrock_model = find_best_bedrock_model(latest_model, models['bedrock'])
386: 
387:     next unless openrouter_model || bedrock_model || models['anthropic'].include?(latest_model)
388: 
389:     aliases[base_name] = { 'anthropic' => latest_model }
390:     aliases[base_name]['openrouter'] = openrouter_model if openrouter_model
391:     aliases[base_name]['bedrock'] = bedrock_model if bedrock_model
392:     aliases[base_name]['azure'] = latest_model if models['azure'].include?(latest_model)
393:   end
394: 
395:   models['bedrock'].each do |bedrock_model|
396:     next unless bedrock_model.start_with?('anthropic.')
397:     next unless bedrock_model =~ /anthropic\.(claude-[a-z0-9.-]+)-\d{8}/
398: 
399:     base_name = Regexp.last_match(1)
400:     anthropic_name = base_name.tr('.', '-')
401: 
402:     next if aliases[anthropic_name]
403: 
404:     openrouter_variants = [
405:       "anthropic/#{anthropic_name}",
406:       "anthropic/#{base_name}"
407:     ]
408: 
409:     openrouter_model = openrouter_variants.find { |v| models['openrouter'].include?(v) }
410: 
411:     aliases[anthropic_name] = { 'bedrock' => bedrock_model }
412:     aliases[anthropic_name]['anthropic'] = anthropic_name if models['anthropic'].include?(anthropic_name)
413:     aliases[anthropic_name]['openrouter'] = openrouter_model if openrouter_model
414:   end
415: 
416:   # Gemini models (also map to vertexai)
417:   models['gemini'].each do |model|
418:     openrouter_variants = [
419:       "google/#{model}",
420:       "google/#{model.gsub('gemini-', 'gemini-').tr('.', '-')}",
421:       "google/#{model.gsub('gemini-', 'gemini-')}"
422:     ]
423: 
424:     openrouter_model = openrouter_variants.find { |v| models['openrouter'].include?(v) }
425:     vertexai_model = models['vertexai'].include?(model) ? model : nil
426: 
427:     next unless openrouter_model || vertexai_model
428: 
429:     alias_key = model.gsub('-latest', '')
430:     aliases[alias_key] = { 'gemini' => model }
431:     aliases[alias_key]['openrouter'] = openrouter_model if openrouter_model
432:     aliases[alias_key]['vertexai'] = vertexai_model if vertexai_model
433:   end
434: 
435:   # VertexAI models that aren't in Gemini (e.g. older models like text-bison)
436:   models['vertexai'].each do |model|
437:     # Skip if already handled above
438:     next if models['gemini'].include?(model)
439: 
440:     # Check if OpenRouter has this Google model
441:     openrouter_variants = [
442:       "google/#{model}",
443:       "google/#{model.tr('.', '-')}"
444:     ]
445: 
446:     openrouter_model = openrouter_variants.find { |v| models['openrouter'].include?(v) }
447:     gemini_model = models['gemini'].include?(model) ? model : nil
448: 
449:     next unless openrouter_model || gemini_model
450: 
451:     alias_key = model.gsub('-latest', '')
452:     next if aliases[alias_key] # Skip if already created
453: 
454:     aliases[alias_key] = { 'vertexai' => model }
455:     aliases[alias_key]['openrouter'] = openrouter_model if openrouter_model
456:     aliases[alias_key]['gemini'] = gemini_model if gemini_model
457:   end
458: 
459:   models['deepseek'].each do |model|
460:     openrouter_model = "deepseek/#{model}"
461:     next unless models['openrouter'].include?(openrouter_model)
462: 
463:     alias_key = model.gsub('-latest', '')
464:     aliases[alias_key] = {
465:       'deepseek' => model,
466:       'openrouter' => openrouter_model
467:     }
468:   end
469: 
470:   add_xai_aliases(aliases, models['xai'])
471: 
472:   sorted_aliases = aliases.sort.to_h
473:   File.write(RubyLLM::Aliases.aliases_file, JSON.pretty_generate(sorted_aliases))
474: 
475:   puts "Generated #{sorted_aliases.size} aliases"
476: end
477: 
478: def add_xai_aliases(aliases, xai_models)
479:   return unless xai_models.include?('grok-4.3')
480: 
481:   %w[
482:     grok-latest
483:     grok-3
484:     grok-3-latest
485:     grok-3-mini
486:     grok-3-mini-latest
487:     grok-4
488:     grok-4-latest
489:     grok-4-fast
490:     grok-4-fast-reasoning
491:     grok-4-fast-reasoning-latest
492:     grok-4-fast-non-reasoning
493:     grok-4-fast-non-reasoning-latest
494:     grok-4-1-fast
495:     grok-4-1-fast-reasoning
496:     grok-4-1-fast-reasoning-latest
497:     grok-4-1-fast-non-reasoning
498:     grok-4-1-fast-non-reasoning-latest
499:   ].each do |alias_key|
500:     aliases[alias_key] ||= { 'xai' => 'grok-4.3' }
501:   end
502: end
503: 
504: def group_anthropic_models_by_base_name(anthropic_models)
505:   grouped = Hash.new { |h, k| h[k] = [] }
506: 
507:   anthropic_models.each do |model|
508:     base_name = extract_base_name(model)
509:     grouped[base_name] << model
510:   end
511: 
512:   latest_models = {}
513:   grouped.each do |base_name, model_list|
514:     if model_list.size == 1
515:       latest_models[base_name] = model_list.first
516:     else
517:       latest_model = model_list.max_by { |model| extract_date_from_model(model) }
518:       latest_models[base_name] = latest_model
519:     end
520:   end
521: 
522:   latest_models
523: end
524: 
525: def extract_base_name(model)
526:   if model =~ /^(.+)-(\d{8})$/
527:     Regexp.last_match(1)
528:   else
529:     model
530:   end
531: end
532: 
533: def extract_date_from_model(model)
534:   if model =~ /-(\d{8})$/
535:     Regexp.last_match(1)
536:   else
537:     '00000000'
538:   end
539: end
540: 
541: def find_best_bedrock_model(anthropic_model, bedrock_models) # rubocop:disable Metrics/PerceivedComplexity
542:   base_pattern = case anthropic_model
543:                  when 'claude-2.0', 'claude-2'
544:                    'claude-v2'
545:                  when 'claude-2.1'
546:                    'claude-v2:1'
547:                  when 'claude-instant-v1', 'claude-instant'
548:                    'claude-instant'
549:                  else
550:                    anthropic_model
551:                  end
552: 
553:   matching_models = bedrock_models.select do |bedrock_model|
554:     model_without_prefix = bedrock_model.sub(/^(?:(?:[a-z]{2}|global)\.)?anthropic\./, '')
555:     model_without_prefix.match?(/\A#{Regexp.escape(base_pattern)}(?:-v\d+|:\d+k|$)/)
556:   end
557: 
558:   return nil if matching_models.empty?
559: 
560:   begin
561:     model_info = RubyLLM.models.find(anthropic_model)
562:     target_context = model_info.context_window
563:   rescue StandardError
564:     target_context = nil
565:   end
566: 
567:   if target_context
568:     target_k = target_context / 1000
569: 
570:     with_context = matching_models.select do |m|
571:       m.include?(":#{target_k}k") || m.include?(":0:#{target_k}k")
572:     end
573: 
574:     return with_context.first if with_context.any?
575:   end
576: 
577:   matching_models.min_by do |model|
578:     context_priority = if model =~ /:(?:\d+:)?(\d+)k/
579:                          -Regexp.last_match(1).to_i
580:                        else
581:                          0
582:                        end
583: 
584:     version_priority = if model =~ /-v(\d+):/
585:                          -Regexp.last_match(1).to_i
586:                        else
587:                          0
588:                        end
589: 
590:     has_context_priority = model.include?('k') ? -1 : 0
591:     [has_context_priority, context_priority, version_priority]
592:   end
593: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: require 'spec_helper'
4: 
5: RSpec.describe RubyLLM::ErrorMiddleware do
6:   describe '.parse_error' do
7:     let(:provider) { instance_double(RubyLLM::Provider, parse_error: 'provider error') }
8: 
9:     it 'maps 502 to ServiceUnavailableError' do
10:       response = Struct.new(:status, :body).new(502, '{"error":{"message":"down"}}')
11: 
12:       expect do
13:         described_class.parse_error(provider: provider, response: response)
14:       end.to raise_error(RubyLLM::ServiceUnavailableError)
15:     end
16: 
17:     it 'maps 503 to ServiceUnavailableError' do
18:       response = Struct.new(:status, :body).new(503, '{"error":{"message":"down"}}')
19: 
20:       expect do
21:         described_class.parse_error(provider: provider, response: response)
22:       end.to raise_error(RubyLLM::ServiceUnavailableError)
23:     end
24: 
25:     it 'maps 504 to ServiceUnavailableError' do
26:       response = Struct.new(:status, :body).new(504, '{"error":{"message":"timeout"}}')
27: 
28:       expect do
29:         described_class.parse_error(provider: provider, response: response)
30:       end.to raise_error(RubyLLM::ServiceUnavailableError)
31:     end
32: 
33:     it 'maps context-length-like 429 errors to ContextLengthExceededError' do
34:       response = Struct.new(:status, :body).new(429, '{"error":{"message":"Request too large for model"}}')
35:       provider = instance_double(RubyLLM::Provider, parse_error: 'Request too large for model')
36: 
37:       expect do
38:         described_class.parse_error(provider: provider, response: response)
39:       end.to raise_error(RubyLLM::ContextLengthExceededError)
40:     end
41: 
42:     it 'keeps regular 429 errors as RateLimitError' do
43:       response = Struct.new(:status, :body).new(429, '{"error":{"message":"Rate limit exceeded"}}')
44:       provider = instance_double(RubyLLM::Provider, parse_error: 'Rate limit exceeded')
45: 
46:       expect do
47:         described_class.parse_error(provider: provider, response: response)
48:       end.to raise_error(RubyLLM::RateLimitError)
49:     end
50: 
51:     it 'maps context-length-like 400 errors to ContextLengthExceededError' do
52:       msg = "This model's maximum context length is 8192 tokens."
53:       response = Struct.new(:status, :body).new(400, %({"error":{"message":"#{msg}"}}))
54:       provider = instance_double(RubyLLM::Provider, parse_error: msg)
55: 
56:       expect do
57:         described_class.parse_error(provider: provider, response: response)
58:       end.to raise_error(RubyLLM::ContextLengthExceededError)
59:     end
60: 
61:     it "maps Anthropic's 'prompt is too long' 400 error to ContextLengthExceededError" do
62:       msg = 'prompt is too long: 209025 tokens > 200000 maximum'
63:       response = Struct.new(:status, :body).new(400, %({"error":{"message":"#{msg}"}}))
64:       provider = instance_double(RubyLLM::Provider, parse_error: msg)

66:       expect do
67:         described_class.parse_error(provider: provider, response: response)
68:       end.to raise_error(RubyLLM::ContextLengthExceededError)
69:     end

72:       response = Struct.new(:status, :body).new(400, '{"error":{"message":"Invalid model specified"}}')
73:       provider = instance_double(RubyLLM::Provider, parse_error: 'Invalid model specified')
74:

77:       end.to raise_error(RubyLLM::BadRequestError)

4: 
5: RSpec.describe RubyLLM::Providers::XAI::Models do
6:   describe '.parse_list_models_response' do
7:     it 'keeps only provider-owned fallback capabilities for Grok chat models' do
8:       response = Struct.new(:body).new(
9:         {
10:           'data' => [
11:             {
12:               'id' => 'grok-4.3',
13:               'object' => 'model',
14:               'created' => 1_777_068_000,
15:               'owned_by' => 'xai'

3: module RubyLLM
4:   module Providers
5:     class Perplexity
6:       # Handles Perplexity Sonar media content.
7:       module Media
8:         module_function
9: 
10:         SUPPORTED_DOCUMENT_EXTENSIONS = %w[pdf doc docx txt rtf].freeze
11: 
12:         def format_content(content)
13:           Protocols::ChatCompletions::Media.format_parts(content) do |attachment|
14:             case attachment.type
15:             when :image
16:               Protocols::ChatCompletions::Media.format_image(attachment)
17:             when :pdf, :document
18:               format_document(attachment)
19:             when :text
20:               Protocols::ChatCompletions::Media.format_text_file(attachment)
21:             else
22:               raise UnsupportedAttachmentError, attachment.mime_type
23:             end
24:           end
25:         end
26: 
27:         def format_document(attachment)
28:           raise UnsupportedAttachmentError, attachment.mime_type unless supported_file?(attachment)
29: 
30:           {
31:             type: 'file_url',
32:             file_url: {
33:               url: attachment.url? ? attachment.source.to_s : attachment.encoded
34:             }
35:           }
36:         end
37: 
38:         def supported_file?(attachment)
39:           return true if attachment.pdf?
40: 
41:           SUPPORTED_DOCUMENT_EXTENSIONS.include?(attachment.extension)
42:         end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: require 'event_stream_parser'
4: require 'faraday'
5: require 'json'
6: 
7: module RubyLLM
8:   # Handles streaming responses from AI providers.
9:   module Streaming
10:     module_function
11: 
12:     def stream_response(payload, additional_headers = {}, &block)
13:       accumulator = StreamAccumulator.new
14:       on_data = handle_stream do |chunk|
15:         accumulator.add chunk
16:         block.call chunk
17:       end
18: 
19:       response = @connection.post stream_url, payload do |req|
20:         req.headers = additional_headers.merge(req.headers) unless additional_headers.empty?
21:         if faraday_1?
22:           req.options[:on_data] = on_data
23:         else
24:           req.options.on_data = on_data
25:         end
26:       end
27: 
28:       message = accumulator.to_message(response)
29:       RubyLLM.logger.debug { "Stream completed: #{message.content}" }
30:       message
31:     end
32: 
33:     def handle_stream(&block)
34:       build_on_data_handler do |data|
35:         block.call(build_chunk(data)) if data.is_a?(Hash)
36:       end
37:     end
38: 
39:     private
40: 
41:     def faraday_1?
42:       Faraday::VERSION.start_with?('1')
43:     end
44: 
45:     def build_on_data_handler(&handler)
46:       buffer = +''
47:       parser = EventStreamParser::Parser.new
48: 
49:       FaradayHandlers.build(
50:         faraday_v1: faraday_1?,
51:         on_chunk: ->(chunk, env) { process_stream_chunk(chunk, parser, env, &handler) },
52:         on_failed_response: ->(chunk, env) { handle_failed_response(chunk, buffer, env) }
53:       )
54:     end
55: 
56:     def process_stream_chunk(chunk, parser, env, &)
57:       RubyLLM.logger.debug { "Received chunk: #{chunk}" } if RubyLLM.config.log_stream_debug
58: 
59:       if error_chunk?(chunk)
60:         handle_error_chunk(chunk, env)
61:       elsif json_error_payload?(chunk)
62:         handle_json_error_chunk(chunk, env)
63:       else
64:         handle_sse(chunk, parser, env, &)
65:       end
66:     end
67: 
68:     def error_chunk?(chunk)
69:       chunk.start_with?('event: error')
70:     end
71: 
72:     def json_error_payload?(chunk)
73:       chunk.lstrip.start_with?('{') && chunk.include?('"error"')
74:     end
75: 
76:     def handle_json_error_chunk(chunk, env)
77:       parse_error_from_json(chunk, env, 'Failed to parse JSON error chunk')
78:     end
79: 
80:     def handle_error_chunk(chunk, env)
81:       error_data = chunk.split("\n")[1].delete_prefix('data: ')
82:       parse_error_from_json(error_data, env, 'Failed to parse error chunk')
83:     end
84: 
85:     def handle_failed_response(chunk, buffer, env)
86:       buffer << chunk
87:       error_data = JSON.parse(buffer)
88:       raise_stream_error(buffer, error_data, env)
89:     rescue JSON::ParserError
90:       RubyLLM.logger.debug { "Accumulating error chunk: #{chunk}" }
91:     end
92: 
93:     def handle_sse(chunk, parser, env)
94:       parser.feed(chunk) do |type, data|
95:         case type.to_sym
96:         when :error
97:           handle_error_event(data, env)
98:         else
99:           yield handle_data(data, env) unless data == '[DONE]'
100:         end
101:       end
102:     end
103: 
104:     def handle_data(data, env)
105:       parsed = JSON.parse(data)
106:       return parsed unless parsed.is_a?(Hash) && parsed.key?('error')
107: 
108:       raise_stream_error(data, parsed, env)
109:     rescue JSON::ParserError => e
110:       RubyLLM.logger.debug { "Failed to parse data chunk: #{e.message}" }
111:     end
112: 
113:     def handle_error_event(data, env)
114:       parse_error_from_json(data, env, 'Failed to parse error event')
115:     end
116: 
117:     def parse_streaming_error(data)
118:       error_data = JSON.parse(data)
119:       [500, error_data['message'] || 'Unknown streaming error']
120:     rescue JSON::ParserError => e
121:       RubyLLM.logger.debug { "Failed to parse streaming error: #{e.message}" }
122:       [500, "Failed to parse error: #{data}"]
123:     end
124: 
125:     def raise_stream_error(raw_data, parsed_data, env)
126:       status, _message = parse_streaming_error(raw_data)
127:       error_response = build_stream_error_response(parsed_data, env, status)
128:       ErrorMiddleware.parse_error(provider: self, response: error_response)
129:     end
130: 
131:     def parse_error_from_json(data, env, error_message)
132:       parsed_data = JSON.parse(data)
133:       raise_stream_error(data, parsed_data, env)
134:     rescue JSON::ParserError => e
135:       RubyLLM.logger.debug { "#{error_message}: #{e.message}" }
136:     end
137: 
138:     def build_stream_error_response(parsed_data, env, status)
139:       error_status = status || env&.status || 500
140: 
141:       if faraday_1?
142:         Struct.new(:body, :status).new(parsed_data, error_status)
143:       else
144:         env.merge(body: parsed_data, status: error_status)
145:       end
146:     end
147: 
148:     # Builds Faraday on_data handlers for different major versions.
149:     module FaradayHandlers
150:       module_function
151: 
152:       def build(faraday_v1:, on_chunk:, on_failed_response:)
153:         if faraday_v1
154:           v1_on_data(on_chunk)
155:         else
156:           v2_on_data(on_chunk, on_failed_response)
157:         end
158:       end
159: 
160:       def v1_on_data(on_chunk)
161:         proc do |chunk, _size|
162:           on_chunk.call(chunk, nil)
163:         end
164:       end
165: 
166:       def v2_on_data(on_chunk, on_failed_response)
167:         proc do |chunk, _bytes, env|
168:           if env&.status == 200
169:             on_chunk.call(chunk, env)
170:           else
171:             on_failed_response.call(chunk, env)
172:           end
173:         end
174:       end
175:     end
176:   end
177: end

(End of file - total 177 lines)
</content>

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module RubyLLM
4:   module Protocols
5:     class ChatCompletions
6:       # Normalizes temperature for OpenAI models with provider-specific requirements.
7:       module Temperature
8:         module_function
9: 
10:         def normalize(temperature, model_id)
11:           if forced_temperature_model?(model_id) && !temperature.nil? && !temperature_close_to_one?(temperature)
12:             RubyLLM.logger.debug { "Model #{model_id} requires temperature=1.0, setting that instead." }
13:             1.0
14:           elsif model_id.include?('-search')
15:             RubyLLM.logger.debug { "Model #{model_id} does not accept temperature parameter, removing" }
16:             nil
17:           else
18:             temperature
19:           end
20:         end
21: 
22:         def temperature_close_to_one?(temperature)
23:           (temperature.to_f - 1.0).abs <= Float::EPSILON
24:         end
25: 
26:         def forced_temperature_model?(model_id)
27:           model_id.match?(/^o\d/) ||                          # o1, o3, o4-mini, etc.
28:             model_id.match?(/^gpt-5(\.\d+)?(-\d{4})?$/) ||    # gpt-5, gpt-5.4, gpt-5.4-2026-03-05
29:             model_id.match?(/^gpt-5(\.\d+)?-pro/)             # gpt-5-pro, gpt-5.4-pro
30:         end
31:       end
32:     end
33:   end
34: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: require 'json'
4: 
5: module RubyLLM
6:   # Manages model aliases for provider-specific versions
7:   class Aliases
8:     class << self
9:       def resolve(model_id, provider = nil)
10:         return model_id unless aliases[model_id]
11: 
12:         if provider
13:           aliases[model_id][provider.to_s] || model_id
14:         else
15:           aliases[model_id].values.first || model_id
16:         end
17:       end
18: 
19:       def aliases
20:         @aliases ||= load_aliases
21:       end
22: 
23:       def aliases_file
24:         File.expand_path('aliases.json', __dir__)
25:       end
26: 
27:       def load_aliases
28:         if File.exist?(aliases_file)
29:           JSON.parse(File.read(aliases_file))
30:         else
31:           {}
32:         end
33:       end
34: 
35:       # Replaces the cached alias map from aliases.json.
36:       def reload!
37:         @aliases = load_aliases
38:       end
39:     end
40:   end
41: end

3: module RubyLLM

5:     # Information about an AI model's capabilities, pricing, and metadata.

7:       attr_reader :id, :name, :provider, :family, :created_at, :context_window, :max_output_tokens, :knowledge_cutoff,
8:                   :modalities, :capabilities, :pricing, :metadata, :reasoning_options
9: 
10:       # Create a default model with assumed capabilities
11:       def self.default(model_id, provider)
12:         new(
13:           id: model_id,
14:           name: model_id.tr('-', ' ').capitalize,
15:           provider: provider,
16:           capabilities: %w[function_calling streaming vision structured_output],
17:           modalities: { input: %w[text image], output: %w[text] },
18:           metadata: { warning: 'Assuming model exists, capabilities may not be accurate' }
19:         )
20:       end

22:       def initialize(data)
23:         @id = data[:id]
24:         @name = data[:name]
25:         @provider = data[:provider]
26:         @family = data[:family]

28:         @context_window = data[:context_window]

31:         @modalities = Modalities.new(data[:modalities] || {})
32:         @capabilities = data[:capabilities] || []

34:         @metadata = data[:metadata]&.dup || {}
35:         @reasoning_options = normalize_reasoning_options(reasoning_options_from(data))
36:         store_reasoning_options_metadata
37:       end
38: 
39:       def supports?(capability)
40:         capabilities.include?(capability.to_s)
41:       end
42: 
43:       %w[function_calling structured_output batch reasoning citations streaming].each do |cap|
44:         define_method "#{cap}?" do
45:           supports?(cap)
46:         end
47:       end
48: 
49:       def display_name
50:         name
51:       end
52: 
53:       def label
54:         provider_name = provider_class&.name || provider
55:         "#{provider_name} - #{display_name}"
56:       end
57: 
58:       def max_tokens
59:         max_output_tokens
60:       end
61: 
62:       def supports_vision?

64:       end
65: 
66:       def reasoning_option(type)
67:         reasoning_options.find { |option| option[:type] == type.to_s }
68:       end

72:       end

76:       end

82:       def input_price_per_million
83:         pricing.text_tokens.input
84:       end
85: 
86:       def output_price_per_million

88:       end
89: 
90:       def cache_read_input_price_per_million

92:       end
93: 
94:       def cache_write_input_price_per_million

96:       end
97: 
98:       alias cached_input_price_per_million cache_read_input_price_per_million
99:       alias cache_creation_input_price_per_million cache_write_input_price_per_million
100: 
101:       def cost_for(tokens)
102:         tokens = tokens.tokens if tokens.respond_to?(:tokens)
103: 
104:         Cost.new(tokens:, model: self)
105:       end
106: 
107:       def provider_class
108:         RubyLLM::Provider.resolve provider
109:       end
110: 
111:       def type
112:         output = modalities.output
113:         return 'embedding' if output.include?('embeddings')
114:         return 'moderation' if output.include?('moderation')
115:         return 'image' if output.include?('image')
116:         return 'audio' if output.include?('audio')

123:         {
124:           id: id,
125:           name: name,
126:           provider: provider,
127:           family: family,
128:           created_at: created_at,
129:           context_window: context_window,
130:           max_output_tokens: max_output_tokens,
131:           knowledge_cutoff: knowledge_cutoff,
132:           modalities: modalities.to_h,
133:           capabilities: capabilities,
134:           pricing: pricing.to_h,
135:           metadata: metadata
136:         }
137:       end
138: 
139:       private
140: 
141:       def reasoning_options_from(data)
142:         data[:reasoning_options] || metadata[:reasoning_options] || metadata['reasoning_options']
143:       end
144: 
145:       def store_reasoning_options_metadata
146:         return unless reasoning_options.any?
147: 
148:         metadata.delete('reasoning_options')
149:         metadata[:reasoning_options] = reasoning_options
150:       end
151: 
152:       def normalize_reasoning_options(options)
153:         Array(options).filter_map do |option|
154:           next unless option.is_a?(Hash)
155: 
156:           normalized = option.to_h.transform_keys(&:to_sym)
157:           normalized[:type] = normalized[:type].to_s if normalized[:type]
158:           normalized[:values] = Array(normalized[:values]).map(&:to_s) if normalized.key?(:values)
159:           normalized
160:         end
161:       end
162:     end
163:   end
164: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: require 'time'
4: 
5: module RubyLLM
6:   module Providers
7:     class GPUStack
8:       # Models methods of the GPUStack API integration
9:       module Models
10:         module_function
11: 
12:         def models_url
13:           'models'
14:         end
15: 
16:         def parse_list_models_response(response, slug, _capabilities)
17:           items = response.body['items'] || []
18:           items.map do |model|
19:             Model::Info.new(
20:               id: model['name'],
21:               name: model['name'],
22:               created_at: model['created_at'] ? Time.parse(model['created_at']) : nil,
23:               provider: slug,
24:               family: 'gpustack',
25:               metadata: {
26:                 description: model['description'],
27:                 source: model['source'],
28:                 huggingface_repo_id: model['huggingface_repo_id'],
29:                 ollama_library_model_name: model['ollama_library_model_name'],
30:                 backend: model['backend'],
31:                 meta: model['meta'],
32:                 categories: model['categories']
33:               },
34:               context_window: model.dig('meta', 'n_ctx'),
35:               max_output_tokens: model.dig('meta', 'n_ctx'),
36:               capabilities: build_capabilities(model),
37:               modalities: build_modalities(model),
38:               pricing: {}
39:             )
40:           end
41:         end
42: 
43:         private
44: 
45:         def build_capabilities(model) # rubocop:disable Metrics/PerceivedComplexity
46:           capabilities = []
47: 
48:           # Add streaming by default for LLM models
49:           capabilities << 'streaming' if model['categories']&.include?('llm')
50: 
51:           # Map GPUStack metadata to standard capabilities
52:           capabilities << 'function_calling' if model.dig('meta', 'support_tool_calls')
53:           capabilities << 'vision' if model.dig('meta', 'support_vision')
54:           capabilities << 'reasoning' if model.dig('meta', 'support_reasoning')
55: 
56:           # GPUStack models generally support structured output and json mode
57:           capabilities << 'structured_output' if model['categories']&.include?('llm')
58:           capabilities << 'json_mode' if model['categories']&.include?('llm')
59: 
60:           capabilities
61:         end
62: 
63:         def build_modalities(model)
64:           input_modalities = []
65:           output_modalities = []
66: 
67:           if model['categories']&.include?('llm')
68:             input_modalities << 'text'
69:             input_modalities << 'image' if model.dig('meta', 'support_vision')
70:             input_modalities << 'audio' if model.dig('meta', 'support_audio')
71:             output_modalities << 'text'
72:           elsif model['categories']&.include?('embedding')
73:             input_modalities << 'text'
74:             output_modalities << 'embeddings'
75:           end
76: 
77:           {
78:             input: input_modalities,
79:             output: output_modalities
80:           }
81:         end
82:       end
83:     end
84:   end
85: end

3: module RubyLLM
4:   module Protocols
5:     # The OpenAI Responses API. Overrides the chat surface of Chat Completions;
6:     # embeddings, images, moderation, and transcription are inherited. Runs
7:     # stateless (store: false) and replays encrypted reasoning so multi-turn
8:     # tool calls work without server-side state.
9:     class Responses < ChatCompletions
10:       include Responses::Chat
11:       include Responses::Media
12:       include Responses::Streaming
13:       include Responses::Tools
14: 
15:       # Reasoning models reject the temperature parameter on this API.
16:       def maybe_normalize_temperature(temperature, model)
17:         return super unless reasoning_model?(model.id)
18: 
19:         unless temperature.nil?
20:           RubyLLM.logger.debug { "Model #{model.id} does not accept temperature on the Responses API, removing" }
21:         end
22:         nil
23:       end
24:     end
25:   end
26: end

## Step 1: Orientation

RubyLLM is a Ruby gem that exposes one interface (`Chat`, `Embedding`, `Image`, `Moderation`, `Transcription`) over many providers. The code lives under `lib/ruby_llm/`:

- **Provider integrations**: `lib/ruby_llm/providers/<provider>.rb` plus optional `lib/ruby_llm/providers/<provider>/` modules.
- **Shared routing/config base**: `lib/ruby_llm/provider.rb`.
- **Shared wire-format base**: `lib/ruby_llm/protocol.rb` and `lib/ruby_llm/protocols/` (ChatCompletions, Responses, Anthropic, Gemini, Converse).
- **HTTP layer**: `lib/ruby_llm/connection.rb`.
- **Model registry**: `lib/ruby_llm/models.rb`, backed by `lib/ruby_llm/models.json` and `lib/ruby_llm/aliases.json`.
- **Error typing**: `lib/ruby_llm/error_middleware.rb` and `lib/ruby_llm/error.rb`.
- **Registration/wiring**: all providers are registered in `lib/ruby_llm.rb:104-116`.

## Step 2: Request routing

1. `RubyLLM.chat(...)` — `lib/ruby_llm.rb:58`.
2. `Chat.new` — `lib/ruby_llm/chat.rb:12`.
3. `Chat#with_model` resolves the model/provider pair — `lib/ruby_llm/chat.rb:84`.
4. `Models.resolve(model_id, provider:, ...)` — `lib/ruby_llm/models.rb:132`.
   - If `provider:` given: `Provider.providers[provider.to_sym]` — `lib/ruby_llm/models.rb:134`.
   - Else registry lookup then `Provider.providers[model.provider.to_sym]` — `lib/ruby_llm/models.rb:153`.
5. `Models.resolve` returns `[model, provider_class.new(config)]` — `lib/ruby_llm/models.rb:155`.
6. `Chat#complete` — `lib/ruby_llm/chat.rb:179` → `provider_completion` — `lib/ruby_llm/chat.rb:295`.
7. `Provider#complete` — `lib/ruby_llm/provider.rb:54` → `resolve_protocol` — `lib/ruby_llm/provider.rb:210`.
   - Routing hook: `Provider#protocol_for` — `lib/ruby_llm/provider.rb:49`.
   - Config override: `config.<slug>_protocol` — `lib/ruby_llm/provider.rb:220`.
   - Default protocol: `Provider.default_protocol` — `lib/ruby_llm/provider.rb:172`.
8. Protocol executes: `Protocol#complete` — `lib/ruby_llm/protocol.rb:20` → `sync_response`/`stream_response` — `lib/ruby_llm/protocol.rb:109` / `lib/ruby_llm/streaming.rb:12`.

## Step 3: Shared Chat Completions dialect and customizations

The shared dialect is `Protocols::ChatCompletions` — `lib/ruby_llm/protocols/chat_completions.rb:6`. It composes:

- Chat: `lib/ruby_llm/protocols/chat_completions/chat.rb`
- Models: `lib/ruby_llm/protocols/chat_completions/models.rb`
- Streaming: `lib/ruby_llm/protocols/chat_completions/streaming.rb`
- Embeddings: `lib/ruby_llm/protocols/chat_completions/embeddings.rb`
- Moderation: `lib/ruby_llm/protocols/chat_completions/moderation.rb`
- Images: `lib/ruby_llm/protocols/chat_completions/images.rb`
- Media: `lib/ruby_llm/protocols/chat_completions/media.rb`
- Transcription: `lib/ruby_llm/protocols/chat_completions/transcription.rb`
- Tools: `lib/ruby_llm/protocols/chat_completions/tools.rb`
- Temperature normalization: `lib/ruby_llm/protocols/chat_completions/temperature.rb:10`

Base protocol methods are in `lib/ruby_llm/protocol.rb:20-90`.

Providers that subclass/customize ChatCompletions:

| Provider | Customization files | What changes |
|---|---|---|
| **OpenAI** | `providers/openai.rb:6-46` | Default protocol is `Responses`; audio/realtime/search-preview forced to `ChatCompletions` (`:15`); org/project headers; `openai_use_system_role` toggles `system`/`developer` role in shared Chat formatter. |
| **Azure** | `providers/azure.rb:6-53`, `providers/azure/chat_completions.rb:8-101`, `providers/azure/chat.rb:8-19`, `providers/azure/embeddings.rb:8-23`, `providers/azure/media.rb:8-35`, `providers/azure/models.rb:8-12` | Azure endpoint shapes (`azure_endpoint` `:17`), deployment-style base, `input` array for embeddings, media excludes PDFs, models URL. |
| **DeepSeek** | `providers/deepseek.rb:6-39`, `providers/deepseek/chat.rb:8-22`, `providers/deepseek/capabilities.rb:7-60` | Text-only content (`format_content` disables all attachments), capability/pricing fallbacks. |
| **Mistral** | `providers/mistral.rb:6-42`, `providers/mistral/chat.rb:8-130`, `providers/mistral/embeddings.rb:8-33`, `providers/mistral/media.rb:8-42`, `providers/mistral/models.rb:9-40`, `providers/mistral/capabilities.rb:7-168` | Reasoning payload modes, `tool_choice: 'any'` normalization, `document_url` parts, custom model parsing, full capability matrix. |
| **Ollama** | `providers/ollama.rb:6-45`, `providers/ollama/chat.rb:8-18`, `providers/ollama/media.rb:8-34`, `providers/ollama/models.rb:8-35`, `providers/ollama/capabilities.rb:7-19` | `local?`; image detail `auto`; static capabilities; text/image only. |
| **GPUStack** | `providers/gpustack.rb:6-45`, `providers/gpustack/models.rb:9-82` | Inherits Ollama dialect; parses GPUStack `items` array. |
| **Perplexity** | `providers/perplexity.rb:6-57`, `providers/perplexity/chat.rb:8-19`, `providers/perplexity/media.rb:8-45`, `providers/perplexity/models.rb:8-38`, `providers/perplexity/capabilities.rb:7-73` | HTML error parsing, `file_url` documents, static Sonar catalog, citations capability. |
| **xAI** | `providers/xai.rb:6-37`, `providers/xai/chat.rb:8-21`, `providers/xai/models.rb:8-60` | Image-enabled content, Grok model metadata. |
| **OpenRouter** | `providers/openrouter.rb:6-67`, `providers/openrouter/chat.rb:8-95`, `providers/openrouter/images.rb:9-68`, `providers/openrouter/models.rb:8-73`, `providers/openrouter/streaming.rb:8-33` | OpenRouter reasoning payload, strips JSON schema `strict`, chat-based image generation, architecture pricing, streaming reasoning details. |

Other providers speak different protocols: Anthropic (`Protocols::Anthropic`), Gemini (`Protocols::Gemini`), Bedrock (`Protocols::Converse`), VertexAI (`VertexAI::Gemini` / `VertexAI::Anthropic`).

## Step 4: Settings declaration and validation

Each provider declares options via class methods:

- `configuration_options` — e.g. `providers/openai.rb:32-39`, `providers/azure.rb:22-24`, `providers/bedrock.rb:38-41`.
- `configuration_requirements` — e.g. `providers/openai.rb:42-44`, `providers/azure.rb:26-28`, `providers/bedrock.rb:43-45`.

Registration wires them into the global config:

- `Provider.register` — `lib/ruby_llm/provider.rb:174-176`, which calls `Configuration.register_provider_options` — `lib/ruby_llm/configuration.rb:25-27`.

Validation happens at runtime:

- `Provider#ensure_configured!` — `lib/ruby_llm/provider.rb:237-249`.
- Class-level `Provider.configured?` — `lib/ruby_llm/provider.rb:159-161`.
- Azure has custom logic: `configured?` — `providers/azure.rb:30-32`, `ensure_configured!` — `providers/azure.rb:40-50`.

## Step 5: HTTP errors become typed errors

1. Faraday middleware is attached in `Connection#setup_middleware` — `lib/ruby_llm/connection.rb:112-118` (`faraday.use :llm_errors, provider: @provider`).
2. For non-streaming, `ErrorMiddleware#call` → `ErrorMiddleware.parse_error` — `lib/ruby_llm/error_middleware.rb:14-16`, `:41`.
3. The provider gets first crack at extracting a message: `provider.parse_error(response)` — `lib/ruby_llm/error_middleware.rb:42`.
   - Default implementation: `lib/ruby_llm/provider.rb:104-124`.
   - Custom parsers: `providers/perplexity.rb:26-40`, `providers/openrouter.rb:27-41`, `providers/bedrock.rb:24-31`.
4. Status code mapping — `lib/ruby_llm/error_middleware.rb:44-70`:
   - `200..399`: ok
   - `400`: `ContextLengthExceededError` if context-length patterns match, else `BadRequestError`
   - `401`: `UnauthorizedError`
   - `402`: `PaymentRequiredError`
   - `403`: `ForbiddenError`
   - `429`: `RateLimitError` (or `ContextLengthExceededError` if pattern matches)
   - `500`: `ServerError`
   - `502..504`: `ServiceUnavailableError`
   - `529`: `OverloadedError`
   - else: generic `Error`
5. Streaming errors are raised via `Streaming#raise_stream_error` — `lib/ruby_llm/streaming.rb:125-129`, which also routes through `ErrorMiddleware.parse_error`. Inline JSON errors are mapped by `ChatCompletions::Streaming.parse_streaming_error` — `lib/ruby_llm/protocols/chat_completions/streaming.rb:47-59`.

## Step 6: Sites that must change to add a new integration

Runtime wiring:

- `lib/ruby_llm.rb:18-34` — add Zeitwerk inflector entry if the slug doesn’t map to the desired class name.
- `lib/ruby_llm.rb:104-116` — add `RubyLLM::Provider.register :newprovider, RubyLLM::Providers::NewProvider`.

Model catalog / routing tables:

- `lib/ruby_llm/models.rb:11` — add entry to `MODELS_DEV_PROVIDER_MAP`.
- `lib/ruby_llm/models.rb:26` — add slug to `PROVIDER_PREFERENCE`.
- `lib/ruby_llm/models.json` — add model entries (or rely on `list_models` refresh and let `models:update` persist them).
- `lib/ruby_llm/aliases.json` — optional cross-provider aliases.

Configuration sources:

- `lib/tasks/models.rake:40-57` — read new provider API key in `configure_from_env`.
- `.env.example` — add env var line.

Test support / matrix:

- `spec/support/rubyllm_configuration.rb` — set `config.newprovider_api_key` etc.
- `spec/support/models_to_test.rb` — add entries to `CHAT_MODELS`, `STRUCTURED_OUTPUT_MODELS`, `THINKING_MODELS`, `VISION_MODELS`, etc.
- `spec/support/vcr_configuration.rb` — add `filter_sensitive_data` for the new API key and any auth headers.
- `spec/ruby_llm/provider_spec.rb:7-120` — add to `api_base_cases` and `config_for`.

## Step 7: Edit map for adding the new provider

Create:

- `lib/ruby_llm/providers/newprovider.rb` — provider class, `api_base`, `headers`, `protocol :chat_completions, NewProvider::ChatCompletions`, `configuration_options`, `configuration_requirements`, optional `parse_error`, optional `protocol_for`.
- `lib/ruby_llm/providers/newprovider/capabilities.rb` — capability/pricing fallbacks for `list_models`.
- `lib/ruby_llm/providers/newprovider/chat.rb` — overrides `format_role`, `format_content`, `render_payload`, `build_tool_choice`, thinking handling, etc. (only if the default is insufficient).
- `lib/ruby_llm/providers/newprovider/media.rb` — attachment formatting overrides.
- `lib/ruby_llm/providers/newprovider/models.rb` — custom `parse_list_models_response` if `/models` response shape differs.
- `spec/ruby_llm/providers/newprovider/capabilities_spec.rb`
- `spec/ruby_llm/providers/newprovider/chat_spec.rb`
- `spec/ruby_llm/providers/newprovider/models_spec.rb`
- `spec/fixtures/vcr_cassettes/newprovider/*.yml` — recorded integration fixtures.

Modify:

- `lib/ruby_llm.rb:18-34` — inflector entry.
- `lib/ruby_llm.rb:104-116` — `Provider.register` line.
- `lib/ruby_llm/models.rb:11` — `MODELS_DEV_PROVIDER_MAP`.
- `lib/ruby_llm/models.rb:26` — `PROVIDER_PREFERENCE`.
- `lib/ruby_llm/models.json` — seeded model entries.
- `lib/ruby_llm/aliases.json` — cross-provider aliases (optional).
- `lib/tasks/models.rake:40-57` — env configuration.
- `.env.example`.
- `spec/support/rubyllm_configuration.rb`.
- `spec/support/models_to_test.rb`.
